summary: Well-structured professional test suite with good separation of concerns. Unit tests cover manifest parsing (JSON/txt), config loading, license checking logic (approved/denied/unknown), report generation, and CLI integration. Workflow structure tests validate triggers, jobs, dependencies, and actionlint compliance. Three parametrized act-based end-to-end cases exercise real GitHub Actions execution. Key strengths: clear mock strategy, proper tmp_path fixtures, good parametrization, and explicit job success verification. Minor gaps: output file writing untested, some manifest parsing edge cases missing (empty files, bare package names), config edge cases not all covered, act output parsing relies on substring/regex matching which could be fragile. All major requirements from task description are tested, including error handling and TDD methodology (per docstring). Would catch most regressions.
